Europe
by Alexander

Jun 9 - Jun 29 · Amsterdam · Prague · Budapest · Milan

Amsterdam

Your Hostel · Jun 9–11
Generator Amsterdam

ATL → AMS
DL0070 · Jun 8 3:35pm → Jun 9 6:00am · Airbus A350 · Seat 40G

🚌 Amsterdam Transit

🎫
GVB Tram/Metro/Bus — tap your bank card on yellow OV readers
1-hr €3.40 · 24-hr €10 · 48-hr €16 · 72-hr €21.50 · No separate ticket needed for contactless
🚆
Schiphol ↔ Centraal (NS Train)
~€5.50 · 17 min · Tap bank card on yellow reader at platform · ns.nl/en
📱
9292 Journey Planner App
Real-time tram/bus/metro routes · 9292.nl/en
Daily Schedule
9
Tuesday, Jun 9
Arrival + Van Gogh Museum
›
6:00
✈️
Land at Schiphol
Clear customs. Grab €50 from a bank ATM in arrivals (better rate than any exchange desk). Tap bank card on yellow OV reader for the train — no ticket purchase needed.
6:30
🚆
Train → Amsterdam Centraal
Intercity Direct, platform downstairs. ~17 min. Then Tram 9 east from Centraal → Mauritskade (Generator).
RouteSchiphol → Centraal → Tram 9 east → Mauritskade stop. ~45 min total door to hostel.
7:30
🏨
Drop bag at Generator
Too early to check in — free bag storage. Coffee at hostel bar, rest in common area.
9:00
🚲
Pick up rental bike ✅ Booked
2-day rental pre-booked. Collect from shop. Ride Jordaan canals — Prinsengracht, Keizersgracht, Herengracht. Just absorb the city.
📍 MacBike Centraal
12:00
🍽️
Lunch near Museumplein
🍴 Eat HereBroodje Bert near Leidseplein — open-faced Dutch sandwiches ~€5. Or grab a herring roll from a canal-side cart ~€4. Avoid restaurants directly on Museumplein — tourist pricing.
12:45
🎨
Van Gogh Museum ✅ Booked · €25
Timed entry 12:45pm. Go directly to main entrance — no queue. 30-minute grace window. Allow 1.5–2 hrs. Get the audio guide (€4 — worth every cent).
📍 Museumplein 6
💡 TipSunflowers and Bedroom are here. The Starry Night is NOT — it's in New York. Don't be surprised.
15:30
🏨
Check in to Generator
Rooms from 3pm. Shower, short rest. Meet people in the common area.
18:00
🚲
Bike to Vondelpark
Grab a takeaway, eat on the grass with locals. Most Amsterdam thing you can do on a summer evening.
20:30
🍺
Generator bar — easy first night
Have a beer, meet people, sleep well. You have a big night tomorrow. Don't blow yourself out tonight.
⚠️ Jet lagYou've been awake since Atlanta. Keep it light — one late night now will wreck you for the rest of Amsterdam.
10
Wednesday, Jun 10
Canal Cruise + Bar Crawl
›
9:45
🚲
Bike to Anne Frank House dock
15 min from Generator. Follow Mauritskade → Prinsengracht. Lock bike near Anne Frank House.
📍 Prinsengracht 263 · Cruise dock
10:30
⛵
Open Boat Canal Cruise ✅ Booked · 1 hr
Departs 10:30am sharp from the dock at the Anne Frank House. Open boat — bring a light layer, mornings can be cool on the water. Returns 11:30am.
⚠️ Don't be lateBoat will not wait. Be at the dock by 10:20am.
11:30
🛍️
Albert Cuyp Market
Bike south through De Pijp. Biggest street market in the Netherlands. Fresh stroopwafels, Dutch cheese, frites. Free to browse.
📍 Albert Cuypstraat
13:00
🍽️
Lunch
🍴 Eat HereFoodhallen — indoor food market in De Pijp, massive variety, ~€10–14. Or graze Albert Cuyp stalls: frites with mayo ~€3, stroopwafel ~€2.
15:00
🏛️
Rijksmuseum tunnel + Museumplein
Ride through the Rijksmuseum's ground-floor tunnel (free, open to bikes). Sit in the square. Afternoon wind-down.
17:00
🏨
Return bike + rest
Return bike to rental shop. Back to hostel. Shower, eat dinner. Bar crawl is 10pm — you need fuel.
19:00
🍽️
Dinner
🍴 Eat HereLeidseplein area — dozens of options €10–15. Try Kantjil & de Tijger for Indonesian rijsttafel (~€25, Amsterdam's signature dish and worth it once). Or a döner kebab near Rembrandt Square ~€7.
22:00
🍻
Amsterdam Bar Crawl ✅ Booked
Check your booking confirmation for exact meetup point. Usually Leidseplein area. Hits 4–5 bars + club entry. Bring €35–40 cash.
⚠️ SafetyPhone in front pocket. Don't accept drinks from strangers you haven't watched poured. Use Bolt or Uber home — not street taxis.
11
Thursday, Jun 11
Fly to Prague
›
9:00
🎒
Check out + head to airport
Check out by 10am. Pastry at hostel café. Tram to Centraal, Intercity Direct to Schiphol. Arrive by 10:30am.
RouteGenerator → Tram 9 west to Centraal → Intercity Direct to Schiphol (tap bank card). 45 min total.
12:20
✈️
KLM KL1355 · AMS → PRG
Departs 12:20pm · Arrives 1:45pm · Confirmation: ZHS***
13:45
🚌
Airport → Prague city center
Buy 90-min ticket at airport machine (40 CZK / ~€1.60). Bus 119 → Metro Line A → Národní třída. MadHouse is 5-min walk.
RouteVáclav Havel Airport → Bus 119 → Nádraží Veleslavín → Metro A (green) → Národní třída → walk to Ostrovní 20.
🇨🇿
Prague
Czech Koruna CZK~$25/day food4 nights
Your Hostel · Jun 11–15
The MadHouse Prague
📍 Ostrovní 20, Praha 1 · Open Maps

🚌 Prague Transit

🎫
PID Tickets — buy at metro machines or PID Lítačka app
30-min: 30 CZK · 90-min: 40 CZK · 24-hr: 150 CZK · 72-hr: 350 CZK
Validate at yellow machine on boarding — fine without: 1,200 CZK (~€48)
App: pid.cz/en/mobile-app
🚋
Key trams
Tram 22 → Prague Castle · Tram 12 → Letná Park · Tram 11 → Vinohrady
Metro B (yellow) from MadHouse → Praha hl.n. main station in 2 stops
🚆
Czech Rail — train info
Your Kutná Hora tour is fully guided — no train ticket to buy yourself
Info: cd.cz/en
Daily Schedule
11
Thursday, Jun 11
Arrive Prague
›
14:30
🏨
Check in to MadHouse
Welcome beer on arrival. Staff will run through tonight's events. Stash bag and get oriented.
15:30
🌉
Charles Bridge
10-min walk from hostel. 30 baroque statues, views of the castle. Best first hour in Prague.
📍 Karlův most
16:30
🕰️
Old Town Square + Astronomical Clock
Watch the clock on the hour. Sit at a café with a CZK 50 beer (~€2). One of the most beautiful squares in Europe.
📍 Staroměstské náměstí
19:00
🍽️
Dinner + MadHouse family night
🍴 Eat HereLokál Malá Strana — Czech pub classics, Pilsner Urquell on tap. ~€8–12 for meal + 2 beers. Very local, no tourist pricing.
📍 Get directions
💡 TonightMadHouse runs group dinners and pre-drinks most nights. Show up — this is how you meet your people for the week.
12
Friday, Jun 12
Prague Castle + Jewish Quarter + Pub Crawl
›
9:00
🏰
Prague Castle ✅ Booked · Valid 2 days
Open from 9am. Ref: GYG83**** · PIN: Z9N***** · Show in GetYourGuide app. St Vitus Cathedral, Royal Palace, Golden Lane. Go early — crowds explode after 10am. Allow 2.5 hrs.
📍 Hradčanské náměstí
Getting ThereTram 22 from Národní třída → Pražský hrad. Or walk up from Charles Bridge (scenic, 20 min uphill).
10:30
🕍
Jewish Quarter Guided Tour ✅ Booked · 10:30am
Ticket: 105*******. Meet at Maiselova Street 15, Prague 1. Keep ticket — scanned at each site. Pinkas Synagogue + Old Jewish Cemetery included.
📍 Maiselova 15, Prague 1
⚠️ ClosuresKlausen Synagogue: closed long-term. Ceremonial Hall + Gallery: closed for renovation until autumn 2026. Open: Pinkas, Old Cemetery, Maisel, Spanish, Old-New Synagogues.
13:00
🍽️
Lunch
🍴 Eat HereNase Maso — standing lunch, Czech meat and bread ~€6. Or svíčková (beef sirloin in cream sauce) at any local pub — Prague's national dish ~€8. Walk one block off tourist squares for real prices.
15:00
☕
Vinohrady café crawl
Tram 11 to Náměstí Míru. Art nouveau apartments, local cafés, zero tourists. Decompress before the night out.
20:00
🍻
Prague Pub Crawl ✅ Booked · from 8pm
Check confirmation for exact meetup. Usually Old Town Square area. ~€20 incl. free shots + entry to 4+ bars. Biggest solo traveler event in Prague.
📍 Meets near Old Town Square
13
Saturday, Jun 13
Petřín Hill + Letná Beer Garden
›
10:00
🌿
Petřín Hill Funicular
Covered by your 90-min or 24-hr transit ticket (40–150 CZK). Lower station at Újezd. Walk down through orchards for free (~30 min).
📍 Újezd lower station
💡 TipPetřín Tower at top (~€6) for panoramic views — only go up if the sky is completely clear.
12:00
🏘️
Malá Strana wander
Descend into the Lesser Town. Baroque streets, hidden courtyards. No agenda.
13:00
🍽️
Lunch
🍴 Eat HereAsk MadHouse staff their current rec. Any local pub one block off the tourist trail: ~€5–8 for full meal + beer.
18:00
🌇
Letná Park Beer Garden — best sunset in Prague
Tram 12 toward Letná. €2 Czech beers, panoramic city view. Locals every evening in summer. Go early for a bench.
📍 Letná Park beer garden
21:00
🎵
Evening out
Cross Club (techno, Holešovice) or Hemingway Bar (craft cocktails, Old Town). Ask MadHouse staff what's on.
🍴 Late Night24-hr pizza and kebab within 1 min of MadHouse — confirmed by the hostel.
14
Sunday, Jun 14
Kutná Hora Guided Tour
›
8:45
🍳
Early breakfast at hostel
Free cereal/toast at MadHouse. Tour departs 9:30am — leave by 8:50am.
9:30
🚌
Sandeman's Kutná Hora Tour ✅ Booked
Booking ID: GYG99****. Guided tour — includes Bone Church + St Barbara's Cathedral. Check GetYourGuide app for exact departure meetup point.
📍 Departure: verify in GetYourGuide app
💡 What to ExpectFull day ~6 hrs. Guide handles everything. Bring water, snacks, comfortable shoes. Bone Church is 20 min inside but genuinely unforgettable — one of the most unique places in Europe.
13:00
🍽️
Lunch in Kutná Hora
🍴 Eat HerePivnice Dačický — historic pub near St Barbara's, Czech classics €6–9, great goulash. Your guide will point you to good spots. Budget €10.
17:00
🚆
Return to Prague
Tour returns you to Prague. Pack tonight — train to Budapest departs 9:37am tomorrow.
⚠️ Pack TonightTrain departs Praha hl.n. at 9:37am. Leave hostel by 8:45am. Set your alarm now.
15
Monday, Jun 15
Train to Budapest
›
8:45
🎒
Check out + head to station
Grab hostel breakfast. Metro B (yellow) from Národní třída → Praha hl.n. 2 stops, ~5 min.
9:37
🚆
Train: Praha hl.n. → Budapest-Nyugati
7 hrs through the Danube valley — genuinely beautiful. Right-side window seats (facing forward) have the best views through Slovakia. Border patrol may walk through — have passport ready.
📍 Praha hlavní nádraží
16:28
🏙️
Arrive Budapest-Nyugati
Metro 3 (blue) south → Kálvin tér → walk 8 min. Or Tram 4/6 east → Fővám tér → 3 min walk to Lónyay u. 31.
Route to MaverickNyugati → Metro 3 south → Kálvin tér → walk 8 min OR Tram 4/6 → Fővám tér → walk 3 min.
18:00
🍺
First evening — Szimpla Kert
Check in, drop bag, walk 15 min to Szimpla. Go before 8pm for a seat. Best first taste of Budapest.
📍 Kazinczy u. 14
🇭🇺
Budapest
Forint HUF~$35/day food4 nights
Your Hostel · Jun 15–19
Maverick Central Market
📍 Lónyay u. 31, 1093 Budapest · Open Maps

🚌 Budapest Transit

🎫
BKK Tickets — buy at station machines or BudapestGO app
Single: 450 HUF (~€1.15) · 24-hr: 3,000 HUF (~€7.60) · 72-hr: 5,500 HUF (~€14)
Fine without ticket: 12,000 HUF (~€30) — plain-clothes inspectors
App: bkk.hu → BudapestGO
✈️
Airport Bus 100E — special ticket, NOT covered by regular transit
900 HUF (~€2.30) · Deák tér or Kálvin tér → BUD airport · 30 min · Every 10–15 min
Buy at BKK machine or BudapestGO app
🚃
HÉV Suburban Rail → Szentendre (Jun 18 optional)
Batthyány tér → Szentendre · ~40 min · ~500 HUF (~€1.30) each way
🚋
Key lines
Metro 1 (yellow) → Széchenyi Baths · Tram 2 → Danube riverfront · Tram 4/6 → Great Market Hall
Daily Schedule
16
Tuesday, Jun 16
Walking Tour + Thermal Baths + Danube Cruise
›
10:15
🚶
Free Walking Tour ✅ Booked · 10:30am
Booking: BK-*** · Ticket: TK-***. Meet at Budapest Eye ferris wheel, next to the fountain, Erzsébet tér. Look for guide with a blue flag. ~2 hrs. Tip ~€10. Tour ends near Parliament.
📍 Budapest Eye, Erzsébet tér
13:00
🍽️
Lunch near City Park
🍴 Eat HereFood stalls in City Park near Széchenyi ~€5–8. Stock up on water — you'll sweat in the baths.
13:30
♨️
Széchenyi Thermal Baths ✅ Booked · Full-day locker
Booking ID: 317*****. Full-day locker from 7am — arrive after walking tour. Bring: towel, flip-flops, padlock (or rent ~€2). Three outdoor pools (38°C, 34°C, lap swim) + 15 indoor. Plan 2–3 hrs. Centrifuge dryer for swimwear.
📍 Állatkerti krt. 9–11
Getting ThereMetro Line 1 (yellow) → Széchenyi fürdő. Direct, 3 stops from Deák tér, ~10 min.
💡 Best tip of the tripStrike up conversation in the outdoor pool. Strangers naturally talk here — best solo traveler social spot on the entire journey.
17:30
⛵
Danube River Cruise ✅ Booked
Check confirmation for exact time and dock. Parliament lit up at sunset from the water is one of the most beautiful things you'll see in Europe.
📍 Vigadó tér dock
Getting ThereTram 2 runs along the Danube riverfront — hop off at Vigadó tér.
19:30
🍽️
Dinner — Jewish Quarter
🍴 Eat HereMazel Tov — beautiful open-air courtyard, ~€15–20, book ahead. Or street food on Kazinczy utca. Lángos — fried dough with sour cream + cheese ~€3 from a stall.
📍 Mazel Tov
21:30
🏚️
Ruin bar night
Circuit: Szimpla Kert → Instant → Fogas Ház → Ellátó Kert. All within 10 min walk. Arrive before 10pm to skip queues.
17
Wednesday, Jun 17
Buda Castle + Great Market Hall + Gellért Hill
›
8:30
🚡
Buda Castle Funicular ✅ Booked
Walk Chain Bridge to Buda side (~15 min from hostel). Lower station at bridge end. ~€5 up. Front car for views. Walk back down stairs free.
📍 Sikló u. lower station
💡 TipFront car only — middle and back are enclosed with no views.
9:30
🏰
Buda Castle + Fisherman's Bastion
Castle grounds free. Fisherman's Bastion lower level free, upper ~€4. Best Parliament view in the city. Go before 10am — very crowded after.
📍 Fisherman's Bastion
12:00
🛒
Great Market Hall — lunch
Tram 4/6 across Liberty Bridge → stops right at the market (3 min from hostel). Ground floor: fresh produce + paprika + salami. Upper floor: street food + souvenirs.
📍 Vámház krt. 1–3
🍴 Eat HereTop floor stalls: lángos ~€3, goulash soup ~€4, kürtőskalács (chimney cake) ~€2.50. Best cheap lunch in Budapest.
14:30
😴
Rest at hostel
3 min from the market. Rooftop terrace if the weather is good. Recharge for the sunset hike.
18:00
⛰️
Gellért Hill — sunset hike
30 min walk up from Liberty Bridge. 360° view of both Buda and Pest, all bridges lit up. Bring a cold beer from a shop below. One of the best free moments of the whole trip.
📍 Gellért Hill Citadella
20:30
🍽️
Dinner
🍴 Eat HereBors GasztroBar — legendary gourmet soup bar, ~€5 for enormous portion. One of Budapest's best cheap eats.
📍 Bors GasztroBar
18
Thursday, Jun 18
Flex Day + Final Night Out
›
All day
🗺️
Choose your day
Option A — Szentendre VillageHÉV from Batthyány tér → Szentendre. ~40 min, ~€1.30 each way. Serbian village on the Danube — cobblestones, galleries, waterfront cafes. Leave 9am, back by 3pm.
📍 HÉV departs Batthyány tér
Option B — Gellért Thermal BathsArt Nouveau bath — more ornate and quieter than Széchenyi. ~€20. 5 min walk from hostel.
📍 Gellért Baths
Option C — Eat and wanderRevisit Great Market Hall for lunch, explore Palace District arts scene (District VIII), day trip to the Buda hills.
20:00
🎒
Pack + final night out
Pack everything tonight. One last round in the ruin bars.
⚠️ Tomorrow — early startFlight FR8325 departs 1:35pm. Be at BUD by 11am. Check out 10am. Bus 100E takes 30 min. Set alarm 8:30am.
19
Friday, Jun 19
Fly to Milan
›
9:30
🎒
Check out + Bus 100E to airport
Buy 100E ticket at machine or BudapestGO app (900 HUF — NOT a regular transit ticket). Every 10–15 min from Deák tér or Kálvin tér. 30 min to airport.
RouteHostel → walk/tram to Deák tér → Bus 100E → BUD Liszt Ferenc International Terminal 2.
13:35
✈️
Ryanair FR8325 · BUD → MXP
Departs 1:35pm · Arrives Milan Malpensa 3:20pm · ~1.5 hrs
⚠️ Ryanair bag rules1 small personal item (under seat) free. Cabin bag must be pre-purchased — check your booking. Arrive 2+ hrs early. Gate closes 30 min before departure.
15:20
🇮🇹
Arrive Milan Malpensa (MXP)
Malpensa Express train → Milano Centrale. Every 30 min, ~30 min, ~€13. Follow signs from Terminal 1 arrivals. You're in Italy.

Travel Tips

Tap any section to expand. Customs, safety, money, and culture for each country.
🇳🇱
Netherlands — Amsterdam
🛂 Customs & Entry
Arriving at Schiphol
›
  • Schengen Zone — your 90-day clock starts Jun 9. No visa needed for US passport holders.
  • Border officer may ask purpose of trip — say tourism, have Generator's address ready.
  • Nothing to declare for personal items — take the green lane.
  • Check if EU ETIAS authorization applies to US citizens by your travel date.
🔒 Safety
Staying safe in Amsterdam
›
  • Pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas is the main risk. Phone in front pocket always.
  • Bicycles have absolute right of way on bike lanes — never walk in one.
  • Red Light District: safe to visit but photographing sex workers is illegal and enforced aggressively.
  • Only use Bolt or Uber — never unlicensed taxis from Centraal Station.
  • Emergency: 112
💶 Money
Paying in Amsterdam
›
  • Currency: Euro (€). Cards accepted almost everywhere including street vendors.
  • Tap your bank card directly on yellow OV readers for trams and trains — no separate ticket needed.
  • Use bank ATMs only (ING, ABN AMRO) — standalone tourist ATMs have high fees.
  • Tipping: not mandatory, 10% appreciated. Round up for great service.
  • Some small cafes are cash only — keep €20–30 on you at all times.
🤝 Culture
Dutch etiquette
›
  • Dutch people are direct — they say exactly what they mean. Not rudeness.
  • Bikes always have priority — pedestrians yield even when crossing roads.
  • Cannabis is decriminalized in coffee shops. Do not smoke on the street — annoys locals and is technically illegal.
  • English spoken virtually everywhere — zero language barrier.
🟣
Prague — Lítačka Card
Buy at Metro station on arrival · tap to expand
›
Prague's rechargeable transit card works on Metro, trams, buses, and the funicular up to Petřín Hill. Buy at any Metro station ticket machine or airport transit desk. Much cheaper than buying paper tickets each time. Each person needs their own card — not shareable.
Card costFree (just load credit)
30 min ride40 CZK (~€1.60)
90 min ride50 CZK (~€2.00) — use this one
24h pass120 CZK (~€4.80)
3-day pass330 CZK (~€13) — best value
Buy atAny Metro station yellow machine
⚠️ ValidateMust validate before boarding — plain-clothes inspectors fine ~500 CZK
📖 Official DPP guide
Prague
💰 Money
Czech Koruna — most important tip
›
  • Currency: Czech Koruna (CZK) — NOT Euro. 1 EUR ≈ 25 CZK. 5,000 CZK is only ~€200.
  • Get CZK from bank ATMs only (Česká spořitelna, Komerční banka).
  • Never exchange at airport kiosks or tourist booths — scam-level rates.
  • Beer at a local pub: 50–80 CZK (~€2–3). Over 100 CZK = tourist trap bar.
  • Tipping: 10% standard. Round up to a convenient CZK number.
🔒 Safety
Taxis & scams to avoid
›
  • Prague is very safe. Main risk is tourist scams, not crime.
  • Taxi scams — only use Bolt or Uber. Never take a street taxi without a meter.
  • Validate your transit ticket before boarding — plain-clothes inspectors fine tourists ~500 CZK instantly, no excuses.
  • Touts near Wenceslas Square offering clubs and "free entry" — ignore all of them.
  • Emergency: 112 · Police: 158
🤝 Culture & Etiquette
Toasts, bills, and local customs
›
  • Greet entering shops: "Dobrý den". Leaving: "Na shledanou". Thank you: "Děkuji".
  • Always make eye contact during a toast before clinking glasses — not doing so is considered rude and bad luck.
  • Waiters don't bring the bill automatically — flag them and say "Zaplatím prosím" (I'd like to pay).
  • English widely spoken by anyone under 40.
🔴
Budapest — Budapest GO Card
Buy at BKK desk or Metro machines on arrival · tap to expand
›
Budapest's transit card covers Metro, trams, buses, trolleybuses, and HÉV suburban rail within city limits. The airport bus (100E) requires a separate ticket — it does NOT use the regular transit system. Buy at the airport BKK desk or any Metro machine.
Single ride450 HUF (~€1.10)
24h pass2,500 HUF (~€6.30) — best for day 1
72h pass5,500 HUF (~€14) — best value for 3 nights
Airport bus900 HUF (~€2.30) — separate ticket for Bus 100E
Buy atBKK desk at airport or any Metro machine
⚠️ ValidatePunch/tap before boarding — inspectors fine immediately
📖 Official BKK guide
Budapest
💰 Money — Read Before Arriving
Hungarian Forint — numbers look huge, don't panic
›
  • Currency: Hungarian Forint (HUF). 1 EUR ≈ 395 HUF. 10,000 HUF = only ~€25.
  • Get HUF from bank ATMs only (OTP Bank, K&H, Raiffeisen). Never exchange at the airport or hotels.
  • Cards accepted in most restaurants but markets and smaller places are cash only.
  • Tipping: 10–15% expected in restaurants. Check if service charge is already on the bill.
  • Airport bus 100E ticket (900 HUF) is separate from regular transit — buy it specifically at the BKK desk.
🔒 Safety — Read Before Going Out
Bar girl scam & taxis — most costly risks
›
  • Bar girl scam: A woman befriends you and suggests a bar. You end up with a €300+ bill you're pressured (sometimes physically) to pay. Never let a stranger pick the bar.
  • Taxi scams: Only use Bolt or Uber from the app — especially outside clubs at night. Never take an unsolicited cab.
  • Thermal baths: bring a padlock for the locker. Don't leave valuables in changing rooms.
  • Emergency: 112 · Police: 107
🤝 Culture & Etiquette
Beer rule, thermal baths, local customs
›
  • Hello: "Szia" (SEE-ya). Thank you: "Köszönöm" (KER-suh-num).
  • Do NOT clink beer glasses. Dates to 1849 when Austrian generals toasted the execution of Hungarian revolutionaries. Locals take this seriously. Wine and spirits are fine — beer is not.
  • Thermal bath etiquette: shower before entering. Swim caps required in some pools. Stay calm — baths are wellness spaces.
  • English widely spoken among younger locals and all tourism-facing staff.
💊 Health
Thermal baths + practical health tips
›
  • Thermal water is mineral-rich — don't drink it. Stay hydrated with regular water, especially after 2+ hours in hot pools.
  • If dizzy in a hot pool, move to a cooler pool or sit on the edge — very common.
  • Confirm your travel insurance covers medical expenses before departure — US health insurance rarely covers abroad.
  • Tap water is safe to drink throughout Hungary.
🌍
General — All Countries
📱 Tech & Connectivity
Staying connected
›
  • Your Airalo EU eSIM covers all three countries. Activate before landing at Schiphol.
  • Download offline Google Maps for Amsterdam, Prague, Budapest before leaving Atlanta. Critical.
  • Power plugs: EU Type C/F (round two-pin). US plugs DO NOT fit — bring an adapter.
  • Voltage: 230V. Most modern chargers handle this (check brick for "100–240V").
  • WhatsApp is standard for communication across Europe.
🆘 Emergencies
What to do if something goes wrong
›
  • EU Emergency: 112 — works on any phone, even with no SIM or credit.
  • Lost/stolen passport — US Embassies:
    Amsterdam: +31 20 575 5309
    Prague: +420 257 022 000
    Budapest: +36 1 475 4400
  • Lost cards: call your bank immediately. Keep backup card stored separately from wallet.
  • Email yourself a photo of your passport bio page — accessible anywhere with internet.
  • Delta emergency line: +1-800-221-1212
Europe 2026 · Alexander Payne · Available offline via Gmail or Dropbox
